CATaN 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • CATaN: Canonical correlation Analysis of Transcriptome and TF-gene regulatory Networks
  • Changelog

Run Canonical Correlation Analysis of TF-GRN matrix and transcriptome data

Source: R/run_cca.R
run_cca.Rd

Performs CCA between a TF-GRN matrix (X) and a transcriptome matrix (Y) via SVD of the cross-product matrix X'Y. Returns a CATaNResult object containing CC sample loadings and summary parameters.

run_cca(
  tf_matrix,
  transcriptome,
  n_cc = 10L,
  n_hvg = 10000L,
  sample_metadata = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

tf_matrix

Numeric matrix of TF-gene connectivity scores (gene x TF). Typically from prepare_matrices.

transcriptome

Numeric matrix of normalised expression values (gene x sample). Typically log2(CPM + 1) from prepare_matrices.

n_cc

Integer. Number of canonical components to retain (default 10L).

n_hvg

Integer. Number of top highly variable genes to select from each matrix before taking their intersection (default 10000L).

sample_metadata

A data.frame or DataFrame of sample annotations (optional). Row names or a column "id" should match column names of transcriptome.

verbose

Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE).

Value

A CATaNResult object.

Details

The analysis proceeds as follows:

  1. HVG selection: For each matrix, gene-wise variance is computed and the top n_hvg genes are selected. The intersection of these two sets defines the gene set used for CCA.

  2. Zero-TF removal: TF columns where all values are zero across the selected genes are removed.

  3. Bidirectional scaling: Both matrices are z-score normalised first across genes (row-wise) then across features (column-wise). Genes producing NA (zero variance) are removed.

  4. SVD: The cross-product matrix \(X^T Y\) is computed and decomposed via SVD. The left singular vectors (U) give TF sample loadings and the right singular vectors (V) give transcriptome sample loadings for each CC.

  5. Parameter computation: Variance explained, singular value proportions, SCF, and canonical correlations are computed.

Examples

set.seed(42)
ngenes <- 200; ntf <- 20; nsamp <- 30
tf_mat <- matrix(sample(0:1, ngenes * ntf, replace = TRUE),
                 nrow = ngenes, ncol = ntf)
rownames(tf_mat) <- paste0("gene", seq_len(ngenes))
colnames(tf_mat) <- paste0("TF", seq_len(ntf))
expr_mat <- matrix(rnorm(ngenes * nsamp), nrow = ngenes, ncol = nsamp)
rownames(expr_mat) <- paste0("gene", seq_len(ngenes))
colnames(expr_mat) <- paste0("sample", seq_len(nsamp))
res <- run_cca(tf_mat, expr_mat, n_cc = 5L, n_hvg = 100L)
#> Selecting highly variable genes...
#>   50 HVGs selected
#> Performing bidirectional scaling...
#>   After scaling: 20 TFs x 50 genes, 30 samples x 50 genes
#> Computing SVD...
#> Computing CCA parameters...
#> Done.
